articles.title: How life-saving HIV therapy has contributed to increased rates of a sexually transmitted disease
articles.description: A study in Health Economics uncovered an unintended consequence of a major medical breakthrough: while the availability of HIV treatments in the late 1990s dramatically improved survival, they also contributed to a resurgence in syphilis, a sexually transmitted infection.

articles.title: Why electroconvulsive therapy remains contested: Families report memory loss, worse quality of life and limited benefits
articles.description: Electroconvulsive therapy (ECT), which involves passing electricity through the brain under general anesthesia to cause a seizure, usually between six and 12 times, is used to varying degrees around the world for patients with depression and other mental conditions. A study in Psychology and Psychotherapy: Theory, Research and Practice reveals that the experiences of families and friends of individuals undergoing ECT have challenged claims that the therapy is safe and effective.

articles.title: How children in rural and urban areas experience different mental health struggles
articles.description: A study in Pediatric Investigation found that rural and urban children and adolescents tend to experience different mental health issues, with those living in the country showing more emotional problems and those in the city demonstrating more behavioral issues.

articles.title: Do drugs that similarly combat a disease have differing effects on quality of life?
articles.description: A study published in Rheumatology & Autoimmunity challenges the assumption that achieving clinical remission in rheumatoid arthritis is sufficient, showing that patient-reported outcomes vary significantly by drug class even when disease activity is comparable.

articles.title: El Salvador holds mass trial for 486 alleged members of notorious MS-13 gang
articles.description:
Human rights groups have warned that the collective prosecutions violate due process and block defendants from accessing legal counsel
A Salvadoran court on Tuesday began a collective trial of 486 alleged gang members, in one of the biggest mass trials under president Nayib Bukele’s crackdown on gang violence through controversial emergency powers.
Prosecutors say the charges against alleged members of the Mara Salvatrucha gang, or MS-13, span more than 47,000 crimes committed between 2012 and 2022, including a weekend that was El Salvador’s bloodiest since its civil war.

articles.title: Heatwaves, floods and wildfires pose rising threat to democracy, report finds
articles.description:
Research shows natural hazards linked to climate crisis disrupted 23 elections in 18 countries in 2024
Democracy is under mounting threat from the climate crisis, with new analysis documenting how elections are increasingly shaped not only by political forces but also by floods, wildfires and extreme weather.
At least 94 elections and referendums across 52 countries have been disrupted by climate-related impacts over the last two decades, researchers found.

articles.title: Nadendla Bhaskar Rao, ex- Andhra Pradesh Chief Minister who led coup against NTR, dies at 90
articles.description: Bhaskar Rao breathed his last at a super-speciality hospital here while undergoing treatment for old age-related ailments, a Jana Sena Party release said.

articles.title: Ex-Andhra Pradesh Chief Minister Nadendla Bhaskara Rao dies at 90
articles.description: Mr. Bhaskara Rao, who served as Chief Minister for 31 days in 1984 after a political revolt against N.T. Rama Rao, died at a private hospital in Hyderabad on Wednesday; his last rites will be performed on April 24

articles.title: Trump extends ceasefire as uncertainty over U.S.-Iran peace talks remains
articles.description: With a two-week ceasefire set to expire and Iran balking at the resumption of peace talks, President Trump said he would be extending the deadline to allow for Iran to "come up with a unified proposal."

articles.title: Trust Trump? Iran’s Doubts Shadow Peace Talks.
articles.description: Iranian leaders fear being burned again by President Trump, who tore up a nuclear agreement reached during the Obama administration after lengthy negotiations.

articles.title: Wemby concussed on hard fall in Spurs' G2 loss
articles.description: Spurs star Victor Wembanyama sustained a concussion on a hard fall during the second quarter of Tuesday night's Game 2 playoff loss to the Portland Trail Blazers.

articles.title: Edgecombe makes history with 30-10 to stun C's
articles.description: VJ Edgecombe had 30 points and 10 rebounds while playing through pain after taking a hard fall on his back early in the game, Tyrese Maxey scored 29 points and the 76ers beat the Celtics 111-97 on Tuesday night to tie their series at one game apiece.

articles.title: Australian Gas Exporter Santos Streamlines Business to Cut Costs
articles.description: Santos Ltd. is restructuring the company’s oil and gas business in order to cut costs, according to an internal notice seen by Bloomberg, after a string of stymied takeover bids in recent years that have raised pressure to increase shareholder returns.

articles.title: Increased Jet Fuel Costs Hit Global Airline Industry (Video)
Sticks to defensive and quality themes amid volatile global setup: Mayuresh Joshi
articles.description: Indian stock markets are showing a strong rebound. Investors are now carefully selecting companies with good earnings and strong finances. Key sectors like power and pharmaceuticals are attracting attention. Mid-cap PSU banks are also seen as promising. Niche engineering and manufacturing firms are expected to perform well. This selective approach is driven by evolving global demand and input costs.

articles.title: House Democrats demand Kash Patel take alcohol test under penalty of perjury after Atlantic report
articles.description: Rep. Jamie Raskin and House Democrats opened an investigation into FBI Director Kash Patel over alleged drinking behavior reported by The Atlantic, which Patel denies.

topStories.description: Investors are now demanding higher borrowing costs from the so-called 'BIF' problem countries.

topStories.description: Beijing has leveraged its strong economic relationship with the US, Israel, Iran and the Gulf to keep its stakes safe.

topStories.description: Hundreds of Afghan refugees who helped the United States fight the Taliban may be sent to the Democratic Republic of Congo after the Trump administration halted their U.S. resettlement, an advocacy group said

topStories.title: ‘Public will punish Congress’: BJP delegation meets EC over Kharge’s ‘terrorist’ remark on PM Modi
topStories.description: The BJP lodged a complaint with the Election Commission against Congress chief Mallikarjun Kharge for calling Prime Minister Narendra Modi a "terrorist." Union Minister Kiren Rijiju stated that such language is unprecedented and demanded strict action and an apology from Kharge and the Congress party.

dailyNews.title: Minnesota allows ‘happy hour’ in nursing homes under new law easing alcohol restrictions
dailyNews.description: Gov. Tim Walz signed Minnesota's "Grandparents' Happy Hour" law allowing nursing home residents to drink alcohol at facility-organized social events.

dailyNews.title: UK inflation jumps to 3.3% in March as fuel prices surge amid Iran war
dailyNews.description: Fuel prices saw their largest increase for over three years, the ONS' chief economist said.

dailyNews.title: European stocks set to open lower as Trump refuses to lift Strait of Hormuz blockade
dailyNews.description: European stocks are expected to open in negative territory on Wednesday as traders assess the Iran ceasefire extension and prospect of peace talks.

dailyNews.title: ICE detains illegal immigrant accused of sexually assaulting minor after hospital parking lot birth
dailyNews.description: Federal immigration officials issued an ICE detainer for a Mexican national accused of sexual assault on a minor found giving birth the parking lot of a South Carolina hospital.

dailyNews.title: US military launches first-ever autonomous warfare command to deploy unmanned systems across Latin America
dailyNews.description: U.S. military launches new autonomous warfare command under SOUTHCOM to deploy unmanned systems in Latin America targeting narcoterrorist networks.

dailyNews.title: Lincoln Riley thinks USC's 'championship window' is open, despite lack of success heading into his fifth year
dailyNews.description: Lincoln Riley says he believes USC football has opened a championship window heading into his fifth season as head coach of the Trojans, despite critics' complaints.

dailyNews.title: Court upholds Texas law requiring Ten Commandments in classrooms
dailyNews.description: Appeals court upholds Texas' Ten Commandments classroom law, but critics say the fight isn't over.

dailyNews.description: Virginia’s approval of an aggressive gerrymander could give Democrats up to four additional House seats as they seek to win back Congress.

dailyNews.description: With Virginia’s vote on Tuesday, Democrats pulled close to even with Republicans, who may have further cards to play in the race to gain extra House seats.

dailyNews.title: Virginia voters approve new congressional map favoring Democrats, CBS News projects
dailyNews.description: Virginia voters on Tuesday approved a new congressional map that would give Democrats an advantage in 10 House districts, leaving just one safe Republican seat, CBS News projects.

topStories.title: Diving into Trump's claims about "nuclear dust" and what it actually is
topStories.description: Over the past several days, President Trump has made multiple statements on the U.S. goal to seize Iran's "nuclear dust." Will Todman, the chief of staff of the Geopolitics and Foreign Policy Department at CSIS, has more analysis.
